description: Owner-level secret configuration (Telegram API hash/ID, user session, HA token, Google OAuth, email password) is stored as secured contact_info on the owner contact. This conflates identity credentials with contact channel identifiers. Target: new entity_info table, Owner entity editable in dashboard, credentials managed at entity level. Contact-level channel identifiers stay on contact_info.
title: Move owner secrets from contact_info to entity_info

description:
Create Alembic migration adding shared.entity_info table parallel to contact_info
but FK'd to entities instead of contacts.

Schema:
  id          UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid()
  entity_id   UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES shared.entities(id) ON DELETE CASCADE
  type        VARCHAR NOT NULL
  value       TEXT NOT NULL
  label       VARCHAR
  is_primary  BOOLEAN DEFAULT false
  secured     BOOLEAN NOT NULL DEFAULT false
  created_at  TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT now()
  UNIQUE (entity_id, type)  -- one value per type per entity

Key differences from contact_info:
- FK to entities (not contacts)
- UNIQUE on (entity_id, type) not (type, value) — entity credentials are
  identity-scoped, not globally unique channel identifiers
- No parent_id column (entity secrets don't nest)

Files to modify:
- New: alembic/versions/core/core_0XX_entity_info.py
title: Create shared.entity_info table

description: Create Alembic migration adding shared.entity_info table parallel to contact_info but FK'd to entities. UNIQUE on (entity_id, type) — one value per type per entity. Key difference from contact_info: FK to entities not contacts, unique on (entity_id, type) not (type, value), no parent_id. File: alembic/versions/core/core_0XX_entity_info.py
title: Create shared.entity_info table

description:
The memory browser lists facts, rules, and episodes but rows are not clickable — there are no detail pages. Users should be able to click into individual memories to see full details.

What needs to change:
1. Dashboard-domain-pages spec needs memory detail page requirements
2. Backend needs GET /api/memory/episodes/{episode_id} endpoint (facts and rules already have individual endpoints)
3. Frontend needs detail pages (FactDetailPage, RuleDetailPage, EpisodeDetailPage)
4. Frontend needs routes (/memory/facts/:id, /memory/rules/:id, /memory/episodes/:id)
5. MemoryBrowser table rows need to be clickable with Link navigation
6. Frontend needs getEpisode() client function and useEpisode() hook

Existing infrastructure:
- GET /api/memory/facts/{fact_id} and GET /api/memory/rules/{rule_id} already exist
- useFact() and useRule() hooks already exist
- EntityDetailPage provides a good pattern to follow
title: Add clickable memory detail pages to dashboard

description:
attached_molecule: bu-wisp-x9gm
attached_formula: mol-polecat-work
attached_at: 2026-03-06T05:09:28Z
dispatched_by: mayor

The module-contacts spec (Requirement: TelegramContactsProvider, Scenario: Telegram full sync) states:
  external_id = "telegram:<user_id>" (e.g., "telegram:123456789")

But the implementation in telegram_provider.py:83 uses:
  external_id=user_id  (just the bare numeric string, e.g., "123456789")

This affects contacts_source_links uniqueness. While it works correctly in single-provider mode (provider column disambiguates), the bare format is inconsistent with the spec's namespaced convention that prevents external_id collisions across future providers that might also use numeric IDs.

**Fix**: Change telegram_provider.py _user_to_canonical() to use f"telegram:{user_id}" for external_id. Note: existing contacts_source_links rows will need a data migration or the sync engine will create duplicate entries on first run after the fix.

**Files**: src/butlers/modules/contacts/telegram_provider.py:83
title: Telegram external_id format mismatch with spec

description:
The contacts module currently only supports Google Contacts as a sync source. Telegram contacts are a valuable identity source — the user's Telegram contact list contains phone numbers, usernames, and chat IDs that enable cross-provider identity resolution and richer disambiguation.

This epic adds Telegram as a second contacts provider, updating the multi-provider architecture and ensuring contacts from both sources are properly disambiguated via phone number and name matching. Telegram-specific fields (username/handle, user ID, chat ID) must be stored in shared.contact_info to support reverse-lookup and Switchboard identity resolution.

The Telethon dependency and user-client credential infrastructure (telegram_api_id, telegram_api_hash, telegram_user_session in shared.contact_info) already exist for the Telegram user client connector — this work extends that foundation to contact syncing.
title: Add Telegram contacts sync provider

description: The test at tests/daemon/test_memory_chain_registration.py:66 hardcodes an expected list of 3 memory chain migration files. After commits 591b0b65 (004_object_entity_id) and c64da353/9e7aac9b (005_predicate_registry), there are now 5 files. Test needs to be updated to include the new migrations. Pre-existing on main — not caused by any polecat branch.
title: test_memory_chain_dir_contains_migrations expects only 3 migration files, but main now has 5

description:
core_014 migrated entity data into shared.entities and re-pointed FKs, but left empty per-butler entities tables in place. These shadow shared.entities via search_path, causing the dashboard entities list to miss entities that only exist in shared (e.g. the Owner entity linked to the owner contact).

Additionally, general.entities is a completely different table (freeform collection items with collection_id, data, tags columns) that collides by name.

Goal: make shared.entities the sole source of identity entities. Per-butler overlays (facts, contact_info, entity_info) continue to reference shared.entities via FK.

Scope:
1. Rename general.entities -> general.collection_items to resolve naming collision
2. Drop empty per-butler entities shadow tables (education, finance, health, home, messenger, relationship, switchboard, travel)
3. Update memory API router to query shared.entities directly instead of fan-out
4. Update memory MCP tools to explicitly qualify shared.entities

Non-goals:
- Changing facts or entity_info table locations (already correctly FK'd to shared.entities)
- Changing the search_path mechanism itself
title: Consolidate entities to shared.entities as single source of truth

description:
general.entities is a freeform collection-items table (collection_id FK, data JSONB, tags JSONB) that collides with the identity entities table name. This shadowing must be resolved before dropping other per-butler entities tables.

Migration (new Alembic version under roster/general/migrations/):
- ALTER TABLE general.entities RENAME TO general.collection_items
- Rename indexes: idx_entities_data_gin -> idx_collection_items_data_gin, idx_entities_collection_id -> idx_collection_items_collection_id
- Update any FK references

Code updates:
- roster/general/tools/entities.py — update all SQL references from 'entities' to 'collection_items'
- roster/general/tools/collections.py — update any references
- roster/general/api/router.py — update any SQL
- roster/general/api/models.py — update if needed
- roster/general/modules/tools.py and __init__.py — update if needed
- tests — update test SQL
title: Rename general.entities to general.collection_items

description:
attached_molecule: bu-wisp-i5axh
attached_formula: mol-polecat-work
attached_at: 2026-03-06T07:40:01Z
dispatched_by: mayor

After general.entities is renamed, drop the remaining per-butler entities tables that shadow shared.entities. These tables are empty (or contain stale duplicates already in shared.entities) and prevent the dashboard entities list from finding entities.

Migration (new core Alembic version):
- For each schema in (education, finance, health, home, messenger, relationship, switchboard, travel): DROP TABLE IF EXISTS {schema}.entities
- Guard with existence checks; downgrade should re-create empty tables matching mem_002 schema

API changes in src/butlers/api/routers/memory.py:
- Replace _fan_out_memory_queries for entity list/detail/search with a single direct query to shared.entities (using any available pool, since shared.entities is accessible from all)
- Entity list endpoint (list_entities): query shared.entities directly instead of merging results from per-butler pools
- Entity detail endpoint: query shared.entities directly
- Entity search: query shared.entities directly
- Keep fan-out pattern for facts/episodes/rules (those remain per-butler)

Acceptance criteria:
1. Per-butler entities tables no longer exist (except general.collection_items)
2. GET /api/memory/entities returns all entities from shared.entities including Owner
3. GET /api/memory/entities/{id} works for any entity in shared.entities
4. Facts queries still work (FK to shared.entities is unaffected)
5. No regression in entity creation/update via MCP tools
title: Drop per-butler entities shadow tables and query shared.entities directly

description:
Memory module MCP tools in src/butlers/modules/memory/tools/entities.py use unqualified 'entities' table references. After dropping per-butler shadow tables, search_path fallback will make these work, but explicitly qualifying as shared.entities is more robust and self-documenting.

Files:
- src/butlers/modules/memory/tools/entities.py — update all SQL: FROM entities -> FROM shared.entities, INSERT INTO entities -> INSERT INTO shared.entities, UPDATE entities -> UPDATE shared.entities
- src/butlers/modules/memory/tools/facts.py — check for any entity joins
- Any other memory module files that reference unqualified entities table

This is a hardening/clarity change, not a correctness fix (search_path fallback works after shadow tables are dropped).
title: Qualify entity MCP tool queries with shared.entities

description:
attached_molecule: bu-wisp-w2yx
attached_formula: mol-polecat-work
attached_at: 2026-03-06T05:08:33Z
dispatched_by: mayor

The backfill engine (backfill.py) does not correctly write Telegram-specific contact_info types to shared.contact_info during sync. Three sub-issues:

1. **telegram_user_id never created**: When a Telegram contact is synced, no contact_info entry with type='telegram_user_id' is created. The user_id is stored in external_id and contacts_source_links but not as a contact_info entry — so reverse-lookup by telegram_user_id is impossible.

2. **telegram_username stored incorrectly**: Telegram usernames are stored as type='other' with label='telegram' (backfill.py:444-446) instead of type='telegram_username'. Additionally, the value includes the '@' prefix (telegram_provider.py:71) but the contacts-identity spec says it should be stored without '@'.

3. **telegram_chat_id entries never populated**: TelegramContactsProvider.enrich_chat_ids() exists (telegram_provider.py:239) to resolve private chat IDs from dialogs, but it is never called from the sync pipeline (on_startup, sync engine, or backfill engine). No code path creates telegram_chat_id contact_info entries for synced contacts.

**Impact**: Cross-provider identity resolution via telegram_user_id is broken. Switchboard reverse-lookup by telegram_chat_id for synced contacts doesn't work. Username search by type='telegram_username' returns no results.

**Fix approach**: 
- Add post-sync hook in ContactsModule.on_startup or sync engine callback to:
  a) Create telegram_user_id contact_info entries from CanonicalContact.external_id for telegram provider
  b) Store usernames as type='telegram_username' (not 'other') without '@' prefix
  c) Call enrich_chat_ids() after sync to populate telegram_chat_id entries
- Alternatively, make the backfill engine provider-aware for contact_info type mapping.

**Files**: src/butlers/modules/contacts/backfill.py, src/butlers/modules/contacts/telegram_provider.py, src/butlers/modules/contacts/__init__.py
title: Telegram contact_info types not populated by backfill engine

description:
attached_molecule: bu-wisp-a0mr
attached_formula: mol-polecat-work
attached_at: 2026-03-06T05:09:15Z
dispatched_by: mayor

The module-contacts spec defines multi-provider support but only single-provider is implemented.

**Spec requirements (not implemented)**:
1. ContactsConfig should accept a 'providers' list (e.g., [{type: "google"}, {type: "telegram"}]) with legacy single-provider compat
2. ContactsSyncRuntime should manage independent sync loops per provider
3. Provider failure isolation: one provider failing doesn't affect others
4. contacts_sync_now should accept provider filter (sync one or all)
5. contacts_sync_status should return per-provider sync state
6. contacts_source_list should list all configured provider accounts
7. Duplicate provider type validation at startup

**Current state**: ContactsConfig has provider: str (single provider). ContactsModule has _provider (singular) and _runtime (singular). MCP tools validate against a single configured provider.

**Impact**: Users cannot sync from both Google and Telegram simultaneously. They must choose one provider per butler.

**Fix approach**:
- ContactsConfig: Add 'providers' list field, keep 'provider' string as legacy compat
- ContactsModule: Replace _provider/_runtime with dicts keyed by provider name
- ContactsSyncRuntime: Create one runtime per provider in on_startup()
- MCP tools: Support per-provider and all-providers queries
- Error handling: Isolate failures per provider

**Files**: src/butlers/modules/contacts/__init__.py (ContactsConfig, ContactsModule)
title: Multi-provider contacts configuration and runtime

description:
core_014 migrated entity data into shared.entities and re-pointed FKs, but left empty per-butler entities tables in place. These shadow shared.entities via search_path, causing the dashboard entities list to miss entities that only exist in shared (e.g. the Owner entity linked to the owner contact).

Additionally, general.entities is a completely different table (freeform collection items with collection_id, data, tags columns) that collides by name.

Goal: make shared.entities the sole source of identity entities. Per-butler overlays (facts, contact_info, entity_info) continue to reference shared.entities via FK.

Scope:
1. Rename general.entities -> general.collection_items to resolve naming collision
2. Drop empty per-butler entities shadow tables (education, finance, health, home, messenger, relationship, switchboard, travel)
3. Update memory API router to query shared.entities directly instead of fan-out
4. Update memory MCP tools to explicitly qualify shared.entities

Non-goals:
- Changing facts or entity_info table locations (already correctly FK'd to shared.entities)
- Changing the search_path mechanism itself
title: Consolidate entities to shared.entities as single source of truth

description:
The module-contacts spec (Scenario: Telegram incremental sync) describes hash-based change detection:
- Provider computes a hash of the current contact list
- If hash matches the cursor, returns an empty batch (no changes detected)
- If hash differs, returns the full contact list

Current implementation (telegram_provider.py) uses a timestamp-based cursor and always returns the full contact list on every incremental sync. The sync engine's contact_versions hash comparison handles dedup, so the result is functionally correct.

**Impact**: Minor efficiency loss — every incremental sync serializes and returns all contacts even when nothing changed. In practice, this is negligible for typical contact list sizes (< 5000 contacts).

**Fix**: In incremental_sync(), compute a stable hash of the sorted contact list and compare with the incoming cursor. Return empty ContactBatch when hashes match.

**Files**: src/butlers/modules/contacts/telegram_provider.py
title: Telegram incremental sync should use hash-based cursor for optimization

description: The contacts module currently only supports Google Contacts as a sync source. Telegram contacts are a valuable identity source containing phone numbers, usernames, and chat IDs that enable cross-provider identity resolution. This epic adds Telegram as a second contacts provider with proper disambiguation across sources. Telethon and user-client credentials already exist.
title: Add Telegram contacts sync provider

acceptance_criteria:
1. module-contacts/spec.md documents TelegramContactsProvider requirements and scenarios
2. module-contacts/spec.md documents multi-provider configuration (provider list or multiple provider sections)
3. contacts-identity/spec.md documents telegram_username, telegram_user_id, telegram_chat_id contact_info types
4. contacts-identity/spec.md documents cross-provider disambiguation scenarios (Google+Telegram merge by phone)
5. Spec scenarios cover: new Telegram-only contact, Telegram contact matching existing Google contact by phone, ambiguous name-only match, Telegram contact deletion/removal
description:
Update the specification in openspec/ to document:

1. Multi-provider support for the contacts module — the provider field becomes a list or the module supports multiple provider configs, allowing both Google and Telegram to sync concurrently.

2. TelegramContactsProvider contract — a new provider implementing ContactsProvider that fetches contacts via Telethon client.get_contacts(). Document the credential resolution (telegram_api_id, telegram_api_hash, telegram_user_session from shared.contact_info), sync semantics (no sync token — Telegram returns full contact list each time, so incremental sync uses local hash comparison), and the CanonicalContact field mapping from Telegram contacts.

3. Telegram-specific contact_info types — document the new contact_info type values:
   - telegram_username: the user's @handle (e.g., '@alice')
   - telegram_user_id: the numeric Telegram user ID (stable across username changes)
   - telegram_chat_id: the private chat ID for DMs with this contact

4. Cross-provider disambiguation — document how the identity resolution pipeline (ContactBackfillResolver) handles contacts arriving from multiple providers. The resolution order (source_link → email → phone → name) already supports this, but the spec should explicitly describe:
   - A Telegram contact matching an existing Google contact by phone number merges into the same CRM record
   - Telegram-specific contact_info entries are added alongside Google-sourced entries on the merged contact
   - Provenance tracking in metadata JSONB records which fields came from which provider
   - Ambiguous matches (e.g., name-only with multiple candidates) skip auto-merge and flag for dashboard disambiguation

Update specs: module-contacts/spec.md, contacts-identity/spec.md, and potentially connector-telegram-user-client/spec.md.
title: Update openspec for multi-provider contacts and Telegram sync

acceptance_criteria:
1. TelegramContactsProvider implements ContactsProvider interface (name, full_sync, incremental_sync, validate_credentials)
2. Provider fetches contacts via Telethon client.get_contacts() using existing credential infrastructure
3. Telegram username, user ID, and chat ID stored in shared.contact_info with appropriate type values
4. Phone-based disambiguation merges Telegram contacts with existing Google contacts into same CRM record
5. Name-only matches with multiple candidates produce ambiguous_name strategy (no auto-merge)
6. 'telegram' added to _SUPPORTED_PROVIDERS; config accepts provider='telegram' in butler.toml
7. Incremental sync detects added/changed/removed contacts via local hash comparison
8. Sync state persisted per provider='telegram' account
9. Provenance tracked in metadata JSONB under sources.contacts.telegram namespace
close_reason: Completed with no code changes (already fixed or pushed directly to main)
description:
attached_molecule: bu-wisp-hq94
attached_formula: mol-polecat-work
attached_at: 2026-03-06T04:17:28Z
dispatched_by: mayor

Add a TelegramContactsProvider that implements the ContactsProvider interface to sync contacts from the user's Telegram account via Telethon.

Key implementation details:

**Provider (sync.py or new telegram_provider.py)**:
- Implement ContactsProvider with name='telegram'
- Use Telethon client.get_contacts() to fetch the user's full Telegram contact list
- Credential resolution via resolve_owner_contact_info() for telegram_api_id, telegram_api_hash, telegram_user_session (already stored in shared.contact_info)
- Full sync: fetch all contacts, return as ContactBatch with CanonicalContact instances
- Incremental sync: Telegram has no sync token API — fetch full list and compare against locally stored hashes (contact_versions in sync state) to detect additions/changes/deletions
- Map Telegram contact fields to CanonicalContact: first_name, last_name, phone → ContactPhone, username → ContactUsername

**Telegram-specific contact_info entries**:
- telegram_username: the @handle (e.g., '@alice') — stored as ContactUsername in CanonicalContact, written to shared.contact_info with type='telegram_username'
- telegram_user_id: numeric Telegram user ID (stable identifier) — stored in shared.contact_info with type='telegram_user_id'
- telegram_chat_id: private chat ID for this contact — requires calling client.get_input_entity() or resolving from dialogs — stored in shared.contact_info with type='telegram_chat_id'

**Disambiguation / Identity Resolution**:
- The existing ContactBackfillResolver resolution order (source_link → email → phone → name) handles cross-provider merging naturally:
  - Phone match: a Telegram contact with the same phone as a Google contact merges into the existing CRM record
  - Name match: conservative ILIKE match, ambiguous results flagged for dashboard disambiguation
- Telegram-specific contact_info rows are added to the matched/merged contact alongside Google-sourced entries
- Provenance tracked under metadata JSONB namespace 'sources.contacts.telegram.{field}'

**Module registration**:
- Add 'telegram' to ContactsModule._SUPPORTED_PROVIDERS
- Support provider config: allow provider='telegram' or multi-provider configuration in butler.toml
- Credential validation: verify Telethon session is valid before starting sync loop

**Sync runtime**:
- Telegram contact sync runs on the same polling interval as Google (configurable, default 15 min)
- Supports contacts_sync_now MCP tool trigger
- Sync state persisted with provider='telegram' in contacts_sync_state
design:
The TelegramContactsProvider should live alongside GoogleContactsProvider, either in sync.py or in a new telegram_provider.py if the file size warrants it. The Telethon client session should be created once during validate_credentials() and reused across sync cycles. The client must be properly disconnected on shutdown.

For chat_id resolution: Telethon's client.get_contacts() returns User objects with id and username but not the private chat_id. To get chat_ids, iterate client.iter_dialogs() and match by user_id. This can be done lazily or as a post-sync enrichment step to avoid blocking the main sync loop.

Multi-provider support: the simplest approach is allowing ContactsModule to instantiate multiple providers and run them in the same sync runtime. Each provider has independent sync state keyed by (provider, account_id). The ContactsSyncEngine already supports this via the provider parameter.
notes: Design decisions: (1) Create telegram_provider.py as separate file - sync.py is already large. (2) Telegram has no sync token, so full_sync returns all contacts with a timestamp-based cursor; incremental_sync fetches all contacts and engine's contact_versions hash handles dedup. (3) Credential resolution via resolve_owner_contact_info for telegram_api_id, telegram_api_hash, telegram_user_session. (4) list_groups returns empty GroupBatch (Telegram contacts don't have groups). (5) Chat ID resolution via iter_dialogs post-sync enrichment. (6) ContactsModule.on_startup gains elif branch for telegram provider.
title: Implement TelegramContactsProvider for contacts sync

1. Every requirement in the epic spec has a corresponding implementation bead
2. Any gaps found result in new child beads created under the same epic
3. Reconciliation summary is recorded in the close reason
close_reason: All 4 gap beads resolved. Reconciliation complete — every spec requirement mapped to implementation.

attached_molecule: bu-wisp-85u5

Deep-dive review: compare the original spec/requirements (see epic description and updated openspec specs) against the implementation delivered by sibling beads under this epic.

Workflow:
1. Re-read the epic description and all sibling bead descriptions/acceptance criteria.
2. Audit the codebase changes delivered by each sibling bead.
3. Produce a checklist mapping every spec requirement to its implementing bead.
4. For any requirement NOT covered (or only partially covered):
   a. Create a new child bead under this epic describing the missing work.
   b. Set appropriate priority and link dependencies.
   c. Do NOT create another reconciliation bead.
5. Keep this reconciliation bead open (or blocked) until all newly created gap beads are closed.
6. Re-run the requirement-to-bead checklist and close this bead only when all requirements show full coverage.
design:
# Spec-to-Code Reconciliation: Telegram Contacts Sync

## Methodology
Compared specs (module-contacts/spec.md, contacts-identity/spec.md, connector-telegram-user-client/spec.md) against implementation (telegram_provider.py, contacts/__init__.py, backfill.py).

## Requirement Coverage Matrix

### module-contacts/spec.md

| Requirement | Bead | Status | Notes |
|---|---|---|---|
| ContactsModule Configuration (multi-provider `providers` list) | bu-s21.2 | **GAP** | Code uses single `provider` string, not `providers` list. No multi-provider config parsing. |
| Legacy single-provider config compat | bu-s21.2 | IMPLEMENTED | Current code IS the single-provider form |
| Unsupported provider at startup | bu-s21.2 | IMPLEMENTED | on_startup() line 401-405 |
| Sync disabled behavior | bu-s21.2 | IMPLEMENTED | on_startup() line 408-409 |
| Google OAuth Credential Resolution | pre-existing | IMPLEMENTED | lines 470-509 |
| Provider-Agnostic Sync Contract | pre-existing | IMPLEMENTED | ContactsProvider ABC in sync.py |
| Canonical Contact Model | pre-existing | IMPLEMENTED | CanonicalContact in sync.py |
| TelegramContactsProvider - credential resolution | bu-s21.2 | IMPLEMENTED | _create_telegram_provider lines 511-560 |
| TelegramContactsProvider - full sync | bu-s21.2 | IMPLEMENTED | telegram_provider.py lines 140-169 |
| TelegramContactsProvider - external_id format | bu-s21.2 | **GAP** | Spec: `"telegram:<user_id>"`, Code: bare `user_id` string |
| TelegramContactsProvider - incremental sync hash-based cursor | bu-s21.2 | **GAP** | Spec: hash-based with empty batch optimization. Code: timestamp cursor, always returns full list |
| TelegramContactsProvider - deletion detection | bu-s21.2 | IMPLEMENTED | No tombstones, engine-level detection |
| TelegramContactsProvider - contact without phone | bu-s21.2 | IMPLEMENTED | Empty phones list |
| TelegramContactsProvider - validate_credentials | bu-s21.2 | IMPLEMENTED | Uses client.get_me() |
| TelegramContactsProvider - list_groups | bu-s21.2 | IMPLEMENTED | Returns empty GroupBatch |
| Multi-Provider Sync Runtime | — | **GAP** | Not implemented. Single _provider/_runtime. No independent loops, failure isolation, or per-provider MCP responses |
| Sync State Persistence | pre-existing | IMPLEMENTED | ContactsSyncStateStore |
| Sync Runtime with Polling Loop | pre-existing | IMPLEMENTED | ContactsSyncRuntime |
| CRM Backfill Pipeline | pre-existing | IMPLEMENTED | ContactBackfillEngine |
| Identity Resolution Pipeline | pre-existing | IMPLEMENTED | ContactBackfillResolver |
| MCP Tool Surface (4 tools) | pre-existing | IMPLEMENTED | Single-provider only |
| Shared Schema Tables | pre-existing | IMPLEMENTED | shared.contact_info |
| Cross-Provider Contact Backfill (Telegram+Google merge) | — | **PARTIAL** | Resolution pipeline supports it, but Telegram contact_info types not written correctly |
| Apple/CardDAV Provider | — | TARGET-STATE | Not current |

### contacts-identity/spec.md (Telegram-specific types)

| Requirement | Bead | Status | Notes |
|---|---|---|---|
| telegram_user_id stored as contact_info | — | **GAP** | Backfill engine never creates telegram_user_id entries from external_id |
| telegram_username stored without @ prefix | bu-s21.2 | **GAP** | Code stores `@username` (with @); spec says without @. Also stored as type='other' not 'telegram_username' |
| telegram_chat_id stored as contact_info | bu-s21.2 | **GAP** | enrich_chat_ids() exists but is never called from sync pipeline |
| Cross-provider disambiguation | pre-existing | IMPLEMENTED | Resolution order works for multi-provider via phone match |

## Identified Gaps (require new beads)

### Gap 1: Multi-provider configuration and runtime
Spec defines `providers` as a list. Code uses single `provider` string. No independent sync loops, failure isolation, or per-provider MCP tool responses.

### Gap 2: Telegram-specific contact_info types not populated by backfill
- telegram_user_id entries never created (backfill doesn't know about it)
- telegram_username stored as type='other' with label='telegram' instead of type='telegram_username'
- telegram_chat_id entries never populated (enrich_chat_ids() never called)
- Username value includes @ prefix (spec says without @)

### Gap 3: external_id format mismatch
Spec: `"telegram:<user_id>"`. Code: bare numeric string. Affects source_link matching uniqueness.

### Gap 4: Hash-based incremental sync cursor optimization
Spec says compute hash, return empty batch if unchanged. Code uses timestamp cursor and always returns full list. Functionally correct via engine dedup but misses optimization.
notes:
Reconciliation complete. 4 gap beads created under epic bu-s21:

1. bu-fxu (P1 bug): Telegram contact_info types not populated — telegram_user_id, telegram_username, telegram_chat_id entries missing or incorrect
2. bu-a6o (P2 bug): external_id format mismatch — bare user_id vs spec's "telegram:<user_id>" prefix
3. bu-j4t (P2 feature): Multi-provider config/runtime — single provider only, spec requires providers list with independent sync loops
4. bu-p4g (P3 task): Hash-based incremental sync cursor optimization — currently uses timestamp, always returns full list

bu-s21.3 is now blocked by all 4 gap beads. Will be closable when all gaps are resolved.

Key findings:
- Core TelegramContactsProvider (full_sync, validate_credentials, list_groups) is solid
- Credential resolution from owner contact_info works correctly
- Identity resolution pipeline is provider-agnostic and supports cross-provider merging
- The gaps are in the integration layer: how Telegram-specific data flows through the backfill engine into contact_info
title: Reconcile spec-to-code coverage for Telegram contacts sync

description:
Contact detail pages (e.g. /contacts/{uuid}) already fetch entity_id from the API (ContactDetail.entity_id) but never render it. Entity detail pages already link back to their contact (EntityDetailPage.tsx:576-595), so the relationship display is asymmetric.

Add a 'Linked Entity' section to ContactDetailView.tsx that shows a clickable link to /entities/{entity_id} when entity_id is not null. Mirror the pattern used on the entity page for its linked-contact display.

Files involved:
- frontend/src/components/relationship/ContactDetailView.tsx — add the link
- frontend/src/api/types.ts — entity_id already present in ContactDetail interface
- roster/relationship/api/router.py — entity_id already returned in the API response

No backend changes needed.
title: Add entity link to contact detail page

description: Extend the current EAV-style entity/fact system into a labeled property graph by adding inter-entity edges. Today, facts describe properties of a single entity (subject-predicate-content anchored via entity_id). The system cannot express typed, traversable relationships between two entities. Goal: Add optional object_entity_id FK to facts so a fact can represent either a property (entity_id only) or an edge (entity_id + object_entity_id). Enable multi-hop graph traversal via recursive CTEs. Update specs, storage, tools, and LLM prompts. Non-goals: No SPARQL/OWL/formal ontology. No external graph database. No inference engine. Stay on PostgreSQL with asyncpg.
title: Transition entity/fact system to labeled property graph

description:
attached_molecule: bu-wisp-0z6w
attached_formula: mol-polecat-work
attached_at: 2026-03-06T05:08:50Z
dispatched_by: mayor

Update entity-identity and module-memory OpenSpec specs to define KG extensions. entity-identity/spec.md: Add requirement for object_entity_id FK on facts enabling inter-entity edges. Document dual-mode facts (property vs edge). Add scenario for typed relationships between entities. Document entity_neighbors() traversal semantics. module-memory/spec.md: Extend fact schema with object_entity_id column. Add entity_neighbors() tool requirement (entity_id, max_depth, predicate_filter, direction). Update fact uniqueness rules for edge-facts. Add optional predicate_registry table spec. Update store_fact scenarios. All backward compatible, no SPARQL/OWL concepts.
title: Update OpenSpec specs for knowledge graph model

description:
entity_neighbors implementation has spec compliance gaps:

1. RESULT SCHEMA: module-memory spec line 940 requires each result to contain:
   entity_id, canonical_name, entity_type, predicate, direction, content, depth, fact_id
   
   Current implementation returns: entity.{id, canonical_name, entity_type}, predicate, depth, path
   Missing: direction, content, fact_id

2. DEFAULT DIRECTION: module-memory spec line 661 and entity-identity spec line 303 both say
   default direction is 'both'. Current code defaults to 'outgoing' (entities.py line 539).

3. ENTITY EXISTENCE VALIDATION: module-memory spec line 948-950 says when called with
   non-existent entity_id, ValueError MUST be raised. Currently returns empty list silently.

File: src/butlers/modules/memory/tools/entities.py (entity_neighbors function)
title: Fix entity_neighbors: add missing result fields (direction, content, fact_id) and default to 'both'

description:
module-memory spec (line 155-160) requires:
- WHEN store_fact is called with entity_id equal to object_entity_id
- THEN a ValueError MUST be raised

Current implementation in storage.py does not check for this case.

File: src/butlers/modules/memory/storage.py (store_fact function, after object_entity_id validation)
title: Add self-referencing edge validation to store_fact

description:
attached_molecule: bu-wisp-9wpa8
attached_formula: mol-polecat-work
attached_at: 2026-03-06T05:43:07Z
dispatched_by: mayor

Create memory module migration adding object_entity_id UUID column to facts table. FK to shared.entities(id) ON DELETE RESTRICT, nullable. Add partial index on object_entity_id WHERE NOT NULL. Add partial unique constraint on (entity_id, object_entity_id, scope, predicate) WHERE object_entity_id IS NOT NULL AND validity = active. Place in src/butlers/modules/memory/migrations/ following existing pattern from 002_entities.py. Backward compatible - existing facts keep object_entity_id NULL. Downgrade drops column and indexes.
title: Add object_entity_id FK to facts table via migration

description: Extend store_fact() in src/butlers/modules/memory/storage.py to accept an optional object_entity_id parameter. When provided, validate that the referenced entity exists (same as entity_id validation). Use (entity_id, object_entity_id, scope, predicate) for uniqueness/supersession of edge-facts. Update the MCP tool wrapper in memory module __init__.py to expose the new parameter. Update the INSERT query to include the new column. Add tests for edge-fact creation, supersession, and validation.
title: Update store_fact() to accept optional object_entity_id

description: Add entity_neighbors() tool function in src/butlers/modules/memory/tools/entities.py. Uses recursive CTE to traverse edge-facts (facts where object_entity_id IS NOT NULL). Parameters: entity_id (required), max_depth (default 2, cap at 5), predicate_filter (optional list of predicates to follow), direction (outgoing/incoming/both, default outgoing). Returns list of dicts with: entity (canonical_name, entity_type, id), predicate, depth, path. Register as MCP tool in memory module. Add tests for single-hop, multi-hop, filtered, and bidirectional traversal.
title: Add entity_neighbors() tool for graph traversal

description: Update LLM prompts to emit relationship facts with both subject and object entities. Files to update: (1) src/butlers/modules/memory/skills/consolidate/SKILL.md - add object_entity_id to new_facts JSON schema, add guidelines for when to emit edge-facts vs property-facts. (2) roster/relationship/.agents/skills/fact-extraction/SKILL.md - add Step 5b for relationship edge-facts where content references another person, resolve the object entity and pass object_entity_id to store_fact. Add examples showing edge-facts like workplace employment, family relationships, friendship links. Update memory classification taxonomy with edge predicates.
title: Update fact-extraction prompts to emit relationship edge-facts

description:
attached_molecule: bu-wisp-85dm7
attached_formula: mol-polecat-work
attached_at: 2026-03-06T05:43:34Z
dispatched_by: mayor

Add a lightweight predicate_registry table to guide consistent predicate usage across fact extraction. Schema: name TEXT PRIMARY KEY, expected_subject_type TEXT, expected_object_type TEXT (NULL for property-facts), is_edge BOOLEAN DEFAULT false, description TEXT, created_at TIMESTAMPTZ. Not for enforcement - for prompt injection so LLM extractors prefer known predicates. Seed with existing predicates from fact-extraction SKILL.md taxonomy plus new edge predicates (knows, works_at, lives_with, manages, parent_of, sibling_of). Add MCP tool predicate_list() returning registry contents. Memory module migration.
title: Add optional predicate registry table for consistency

design:
# Spec-to-Code Coverage Audit — Knowledge Graph Transition (bu-xd2)

## Updated Audit (2026-03-06, session 2)

All 5 gaps from session 1 have been addressed:

### GAP 1: Prompt/skill updates — FIXED by bu-xd2.5
consolidate/SKILL.md and fact-extraction/SKILL.md now include object_entity_id,
edge-fact guidelines, Step 5b, edge predicate taxonomy, and examples.

### GAP 2: entity_neighbors result schema — FIXED in this branch
Added direction, content, fact_id to CTE SELECT and result mapping.

### GAP 3: entity_neighbors default direction — FIXED in this branch
Changed default from 'outgoing' to 'both' per spec.

### GAP 4: entity_neighbors entity existence validation — FIXED in this branch
Added fetchval check before traversal query; raises ValueError if not found.

### GAP 5: store_fact self-referencing edge rejection — FIXED in this branch
Added entity_id == object_entity_id check before DB validation; raises ValueError.

## Full Coverage Checklist (all green)

| # | Requirement | Bead | Status |
|---|------------|------|--------|
| 1 | object_entity_id column on facts | bu-xd2.2 | COVERED |
| 2 | Property-fact uniqueness index | bu-xd2.2 | COVERED |
| 3 | Edge-fact uniqueness index | bu-xd2.2 | COVERED |
| 4 | store_fact accepts object_entity_id | bu-xd2.3 | COVERED |
| 5 | store_fact validates object_entity_id exists | bu-xd2.3 | COVERED |
| 6 | store_fact requires entity_id when object_entity_id set | bu-xd2.3 | COVERED |
| 7 | store_fact rejects self-referencing edges | THIS BRANCH | COVERED |
| 8 | Edge-fact supersession keyed correctly | bu-xd2.3 | COVERED |
| 9 | entity_neighbors tool with recursive CTE | bu-xd2.4 | COVERED |
| 10 | entity_neighbors registered as MCP tool | bu-xd2.4 | COVERED |
| 11 | entity_neighbors outgoing/incoming/both | bu-xd2.4 | COVERED |
| 12 | entity_neighbors default direction 'both' | THIS BRANCH | COVERED |
| 13 | entity_neighbors predicate_filter | bu-xd2.4 | COVERED |
| 14 | entity_neighbors max_depth clamping | bu-xd2.4 | COVERED |
| 15 | entity_neighbors cycle detection | bu-xd2.4 | COVERED |
| 16 | entity_neighbors validity='active' | bu-xd2.4 | COVERED |
| 17 | entity_neighbors result schema (direction, content, fact_id) | THIS BRANCH | COVERED |
| 18 | entity_neighbors validates entity existence | THIS BRANCH | COVERED |
| 19 | predicate_registry table + seed data | bu-xd2.6 | COVERED |
